name: refactor-assistant
description: Automated code refactoring suggestions and implementation.
capabilities: Analyze code for smells and complexity issues
Suggest Extract Method and Function refactoring patterns
Identify naming inconsistencies and SOLID violations
Propose incremental refactoring with risk assessment
Apply patterns like Extract Class and Remove Dead Code
author: CuriousLearner
securitySummary: Pure prompt-based skill with no code execution, file system access, network calls, or environment variable reads. Contains only AI instructions for code refactoring assistance. All 15 static findings are false positives caused by aggressive pattern matching on benign documentation text.
valueStatement: Codebases become harder to maintain as technical debt accumulates over time. This skill provides systematic refactoring suggestions and helps implement improvements while preserving existing functionality.
limitations: Cannot modify files without explicit user approval
Requires existing test coverage for safe refactoring
Does not execute code or run tests automatically

useCases.title: Modernize Legacy Code
useCases.description: Improve maintainability of aging codebases through systematic refactoring patterns

useCases.title: Enforce Code Standards
useCases.description: Apply consistent refactoring patterns across team codebases for better quality

useCases.title: Learn Refactoring Techniques
useCases.description: Understand refactoring patterns through AI-guided examples and explanations

promptTemplates.title: Basic Refactor Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Refactor this code and suggest improvements for better maintainability

promptTemplates.title: Focus on Complexity
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this code for high cyclomatic complexity and suggest ways to simplify the logic

promptTemplates.title: Find Code Smells
promptTemplates.prompt: Identify code smells in this code including duplicate code, long functions, and naming issues

promptTemplates.title: Apply Patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply Extract Method, Extract Class, and Remove Dead Code patterns where appropriate

bestPractices: Always verify tests pass after each refactoring change
Make small incremental changes instead of large rewrites
Get explicit approval before making structural changes
antiPatterns: Changing code behavior while refactoring structure
Attempting to refactor untested or unstable code

faq.question: What programming languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: Works with any programming language. Refactoring patterns apply broadly across JavaScript, Python, Java, Go, and other languages.

faq.question: What size codebases can this skill handle?
faq.answer: Can analyze anything from single files to entire directories. Larger codebases benefit from focusing on specific areas first.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It suggests refactoring and waits for your approval before making any changes to your codebase.

faq.question: How does the skill assess refactoring quality?
faq.answer: Uses established patterns from Martin Fowler's refactoring catalog and SOLID design principles to evaluate code improvements.

faq.question: Is refactoring safe for production code?
faq.answer: Safety depends on test coverage. The skill recommends ensuring tests exist before refactoring and running them after changes.

faq.question: How is this different from linters and formatters?
faq.answer: Linters fix style and syntax. This skill improves code structure and design patterns through systematic refactoring transformations.
